message:
Improve large JS file parsing: null-terminated buffers and memory increase

- Add readFileNullTerminated() helper that ensures JS source buffers
  have a trailing null byte (QuickJS internally expects this for some
  parsing operations)
- Update all file reading locations to use null-terminated buffers
- Increase WASM initial memory from 256MB to 2GB for parsing large
  bundles like Claude Code (14MB, 116K identifiers)
- Increase stack size from 8MB to 16MB for deep recursion
- Update patches/001-quickjs-utf8-bounds-check.patch (work in progress)

This is part of ongoing work to support large JavaScript bundles.
The Claude Code bundle still fails to parse - further investigation needed.
